model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: This projects as a near coin-flip on overall power: Alabama is slightly higher on KenPom and has the game’s best offense (top-3 AdjO), which can be decisive if the pace rises and it becomes a shot-making contest. Texas Tech’s defense is the best unit on the floor and Christian Anderson is confirmed available, but the season-ending loss of JT Toppin still narrows Tech’s margin for error against an Alabama team that can score in bunches. Texas Tech has a very credible path if it controls tempo and forces Alabama into tougher half-court possessions, so confidence stays low.

reasoningStep.title: Midwest R32 — Alabama over Texas Tech (low-confidence)
reasoningStep.summary: Alabama’s offensive ceiling and slight overall ratings edge outweigh Texas Tech’s defensive advantage, with Tech’s missing frontcourt piece (Toppin) keeping the upset path real but narrower.
evidence: KenPom: Alabama #16 (AdjO rank #3, AdjEM 25.64) vs Texas Tech #19 (AdjD 99.9, AdjEM 25.19).
Torvik: Alabama #17 (AdjO rank #3) vs Texas Tech #12 (AdjD rank #27).
Availability: Texas Tech PG Christian Anderson reported available for the NCAA Tournament (Lubbock Avalanche-Journal, Mar 14, 2026).
Context: Texas Tech is without JT Toppin for the season (torn ACL), reducing frontcourt margin for error versus an elite offense.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.lubbockonline.com/story/sports/college/red-raiders/2026/03/14/christian-anderson-texas-tech-basketball-ncaa-tournament-injury-status/89163753007/ and extracted 1125 characters of text.

result.text: Will Texas Tech basketball's Christian Anderson be ready for NCAA Tournament? Skip to main content Home High School Red Raiders SportsbookWire MMA Junkie Golf Week Texas Tech announces Christian Anderson's injury status for NCAA Tournament Nathan Giese Lubbock Avalanche-Journal The Texas Tech basketball program announced late Saturday night that All-American point guard Christian Anderson will be available for the NCAA Tournament. The announcement comes on the heels of Anderson's early exit from the Red Raiders' loss to Iowa State in the quarterfinals of the Big 12 Tournament on Thursday. Anderson slipped on the ASB GlassFloor LED court the conference was using through the quarterfinal ― the Big 12 moved back to a hardwood floor for the semifinals and finals. Anderson, a first-team all-Big 12 selection and a third team All-American by The Sporting News, is averaging 18.9 points and 7.6 assisters per game. Facebook Twitter Email Help Terms of Service Subscription Terms & Conditions Privacy Policy Site Map Accessibility Our Ethical Principles Responsible Disclosure Your Privacy Choices © Copyright Gannett 2026

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
